summary: Any authenticated member of a course can list and download every uploaded file in that course, even though upload creation and deletion are restricted to course staff. A student in the course can therefore retrieve staff-managed files and other course attachments without a staff-only authorization check.

evidence_snippets.reason: Upload creation is explicitly limited to course staff, establishing a stronger staff-managed trust boundary for uploaded files.
File listing and download are available to any course member, not only to staff or to the original uploader.

evidence_snippets.code:
Uploads in {{ course['title'] }}

New upload

{% for upload in uploads %}

{{ upload['original_name'] }}

Uploaded by {{ upload['display_name'] }}

Download
evidence_snippets.reason: The rendered upload list exposes file names and download actions to any user who reaches the member-only upload listing page.

root_cause: The application enforces staff-only authorization for upload creation and deletion but uses only a generic course-member check for listing and downloading uploaded files, collapsing the intended staff/member access boundary.
repair_plan: Decide which uploads are actually intended to be student-visible and enforce that rule server-side. Restrict listing and download to staff by default, or add an explicit visibility/ownership policy for each upload and verify that policy before returning metadata or file contents. The page should render only the actions allowed for the current user.
